title: Luhut Proposes $1 Billion Joint Investment Between Indonesia’s Danantara and China’s Sovereign Wealth Entities

title: IDX Urges Delisting of Sritex After Bankruptcy, CEO’s Arrest
body: Sritex (SRIL) was declared bankrupt in October 2024, and its former CEO, Iwan Setiawan Lukminto, was arrested earlier this week.

title: China’s Huayou to Break Ground on Indonesian EV Battery Project before August
body: Huayou officially replaces LG Energy Solution in Indonesia's EV battery ecosystem megaproject, Energy Minister Bahlil Lahadalia says.

title: Mongolia in Talks to Export Halal Meat to Indonesia
body: Indonesia still relies on foreign supplies for its meat, and Mongolia is seeing dollar signs.

title: Indonesia to Issue SDG Bond So It Doesn’t Rely on Foreign Investors for Housing Program
body: President's younger brother Hashim Djojohadikusumo says that he has spoken with Finance Minister Sri Mulyani about the SDG bond plans.

title: Prabowo Vows Energy Reform, Threatens to Fire Officials Blocking Investment
body: Prabowo pushes energy reform, threatens to fire slow officials, backs new investments to slash $40B annual import bill.

title: Bank Indonesia Cuts 2025 Growth Forecast After Weak Q1 Performance
body: Bank Indonesia cuts 2025 growth forecast to 4.6–5.4 pct due to weak Q1 data and global slowdown, urges stronger domestic demand policies.

title: It Can Be Hard for ASEAN to Set Common Negotiation Plan on US Tariffs
body: An economist says that each ASEAN member is at a different stage of development, making a common US tariff negotiation plan impossible.

title: Bank Indonesia Cuts Benchmark Rate by 25 Basis Points to 5.5 Pct
body: Bank Indonesia cuts benchmark rate by 25 bps to 5.5% to support growth, citing stable inflation and manageable rupiah pressures.

title: Sell in May? JCI’s Losing Streak Gives Investors Reason to Pause
body: JCI’s May slump mirrors the ‘Sell in May and Go Away’ trend, with analysts urging caution amid repeated mid-year market weakness.

title: Indonesia Revives Boeing-Garuda Deal Amid Tariff Talks

body: Indonesia plans a levy on whole coconut exports to ease local supply woes as prices soar and processors face raw material shortages.

title: Prabowo Appoints New Tax and Customs Chiefs as Revenue Slides
body: Bimo Wijayanto, a senior bureaucrat at the Coordinating Ministry for the Economy, will replace Suryo Utomo as Director General of Taxes.

title: Indonesia Aims to Cut Poverty Rate Below 7.5 Pct by 2026
body: Indonesia aims to reduce its poverty rate to between 7 and 8 percent in 2025, and further to between 6.5 and 7.5 percent by 2026.

title: ‘Preman’: Indonesian Thugs Become Investors’ Worst Nightmare
body: Indonesia is trying to be a haven for investors from home and abroad, but the resource-rich country has a ticking time bomb: thugs.

title: Indonesia Maintains 5.2 Pct Growth Ambition Despite Q1 Slowdown, IMF Downgrade
body: Indonesia aims for 5.2–5.8% growth in 2026, with a long-term goal of 8% by 2029, despite slowing GDP and global economic headwinds.

title: Indonesia’s Foreign Debt Rises to $430 Billion in Q1 2025, Driven by Government Borrowing
